---
# Mask every IPv4 address in raw log lines with replex for safe sharing

**Dataset**: `gx_09_logs`

A log-anonymization / data-sanitization recipe useful when exporting evidence for sharing outside the trusted boundary. `replex` applies a regex find-and-replace across the whole `line`, swapping every IPv4 match (`\d+\.\d+\.\d+\.\d+`) for the literal placeholder `x.x.x.x`. Unlike `replace`, which only does literal substring substitution, `replex` is regex-aware and replaces all occurrences in one pass — ideal for redacting an unknown number of addresses per line. The original `line` is kept alongside `masked` so an analyst can verify the redaction before handing off the sanitized column.

```sql
dataset = gx_09_logs
| alter masked = replex(line, "\d+\.\d+\.\d+\.\d+", "x.x.x.x")
| fields line, masked
```
